content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob are walking on an infinite straight street. Initially, both are at the position X=0X=0X=0 and they start walking in the direction of increasing XXX. After NNN seconds, they stop. Let's denote Alice's speed and Bob's speed during the iii-th of these seconds by AiA_iAi‚Äã and BiB_iBi‚Äã respectively.

Sometimes, Alice and Bob walk together, i.e. with the same speed side by side. Let's define the weird distance as the total distance they walk this way. Find this weird distance.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the total weird distance. It can be proved that this distance is an integer.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

Alice and Bob walk side by side during the first second, from X=0X=0X=0 to X=1X=1X=1.
Then, Alice starts walking faster than Bob, so they do not walk side by side during second 222. At the end of second 222, Alice is at X=4X=4X=4, while Bob is at X=3X=3X=3.
During the next second, they again do not walk side by side, but Bob walks faster, so they both end up at X=7X=7X=7.
During the last second, they both walk side by side and the distance they walk is 444.
Alice and Bob walk side by side during the 111-st and 444-th second and the total weird distance they travel is 1+4=51+4=51+4=5.

Example case 2:

First, Alice walks with speed 222 and Bob walks with speed 333, so they do not walk side by side. Alice ends up at X=2X=2X=2, while Bob ends up at X=3X=3X=3 at the end of the 111-st second.
Then, Alice walks with speed 333 and Bob walks with speed 222, so they do not walk side by side either.
Although Alice and Bob both end up at X=5X=5X=5 at the end of the 222-nd second, the weird distance is 000.

Example case 3: We can see that Alice and Bob always walk together, so the weird distance is 3+3=63+3=63+3=6.
